trade.summary: Bilateral trade has grown steadily. India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, textiles, gems and jewellery, and chemicals to Italy. Italy exports machinery, luxury goods, chemicals, and high-end manufactured products. Italy is a significant market for Indian textiles and IT services.
disputes: Enrica Lexie marine incident (Italian marines case) was resolved in 2021 through arbitration

diplomatic.summary: India-Italy relations were severely strained by the Enrica Lexie incident (2012) where Italian marines shot dead Indian fishermen. After years of legal wrangling, an international arbitration tribunal ruled in 2021 and Italy paid compensation, effectively resolving the dispute. Relations have normalised since. Italy is an important European partner for India in trade and culture.

military.summary: Limited military cooperation. Some defence industry contacts. No formal bilateral defence agreements. Italy participates in UN peacekeeping missions where India is also present.

financial.summary: Italian FDI in India spans automotive (FCA/Stellantis through historical Fiat presence), luxury goods, and manufacturing. Indian IT companies serve Italian corporate clients. Bilateral investment flows are growing but not dominant.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. ENI has some upstream interests. India and Italy cooperate on renewable energy technology.

scientific.summary: Good scientific and cultural cooperation. Indian and Italian universities have exchange agreements. Collaboration in physics, space sciences (ASI-ISRO cooperation), and heritage conservation. The Italian Institute of Culture in India promotes cultural exchanges.
